what_it_does: Documents and supplies an additive patch to an existing content-router edge function: a one-line if-check that intercepts /oauth/consent before existing routes, plus a serveConsent(req) function that returns an HTML+JS OAuth consent page. The consent page loads supabase-js from a CDN, lets the user sign in via email or phone OTP, then calls sb.auth.oauth.approveAuthorization(authId) and redirects to the returned redirect_url.
observations: Top comment gives precise insertion instructions: BLOCK 1 after the pathParts split, BLOCK 2 after Deno.serve's closing })
Asserts existing /r/, /p/, /app/ routes and the 'Invalid URL format' fallback are untouched
serveConsent builds an inline HTML page titled 'Authorize Vi' branded 'Vi (Claude Design)' requesting access to the user's 'design' channel and design submissions
Reads SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from env; references supabaseUrl as an outer-scope variable (not defined in this file)
Supports two login methods (email/phone) via signInWithOtp and verifyOtp, with a 6-digit code flow
standouts: It is a patch-as-documentation file: the actual injected if-check lives only inside comments (BLOCK 1), while BLOCK 2 is live code
Implements the OAuth consent/approval UI for the Supabase-as-Authorization-Server flow
conventions: Follows an additive-patch discipline (intercept before existing routes, leave fallbacks intact)
Inlines all CSS/JS into a single HTML string served from an edge function
Depends on an external CDN script rather than bundling supabase-js
concerns: References supabaseUrl which is not declared in this file — only valid when pasted into the content-router that defines it
BLOCK 1 exists only as commented text, so this file alone is not directly runnable as the full patch
Loads supabase-js from a third-party CDN inside an auth consent page
notes: An additive OAuth-consent patch for the content-router: a commented if-check plus a live serveConsent() that renders an email/phone-OTP 'Authorize Vi' approval page.
questions: Where is supabaseUrl defined in the target content-router file?
Has BLOCK 1 actually been applied to the live content-router, or is this still pending?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header states it is the approved additive consent patch and the file delivers exactly the consent interception and serveConsent function described.
intention_signals: Aims to give the Supabase OAuth Authorization Server a user-facing consent screen so a user can sign in and explicitly approve Vi's access to their design channel, slotted into the existing router without disturbing other routes.
novelty: The patch is self-describing via comments specifying exact insertion points; the consent page combines dual email/phone OTP login with OAuth approval in one inline page.
contribution: Contributes the OAuth consent-screen UI and the approveAuthorization handoff for the Claude Design connector, plus the precise additive-insertion recipe into content-router.
summary_for_embedding: An additive content-router patch providing an /oauth/consent interceptor and a serveConsent() function that renders an inline email/phone-OTP 'Authorize Vi' page calling Supabase oauth.approveAuthorization.
intention_for_embedding: To add a user consent/approval screen to the Supabase OAuth flow so users can sign in and authorize Vi's access to their design channel via the existing edge router.
